description: This figure characterizes the microstructural and phase evolution of the novel Fe-Mn-Al-C-Cr duplex low-density steel after cold rolling (CR) followed by annealing at 600–1050 °C for 5 min. Subplot (a) shows XRD patterns with diffraction angle 2θ ranging from 35° to 80° for CR state and samples annealed at 600 °C, 700 °C, 800 °C, 900 °C, 950 °C, 1000 °C, 1050 °C, identifying FCC austenite, BCC ferrite, Cr₇C₃ carbide, κ-carbide, and β phase, revealing that β phase vanishes at higher annealing temperatures, Cr₇C₃ and κ-carbide form at 950–1000 °C, and all carbides dissolve at 1050 °C. Subplot (b) presents TEM results for DP950-5 (annealed at 950 °C for 5 min): bright-field image, Cr EDS mapping, and SAED pattern confirming grain boundary precipitates are Cr₇C₃. Subplot (c) shows TEM characterization for DP1000-5 (annealed at 1000 °C for 5 min): bright-field images at different magnifications, SAED patterns of ferrite and austenite containing intragranular nano κ-carbides with size of 0.86±0.20 nm and volume fraction of 1.56%. It verifies that annealing temperature regulates phase composition and precipitation behavior, and 1000 °C annealing introduces beneficial intragranular nano κ-carbides without harmful coarse grain boundary carbides, contributing to excellent strength-ductility synergy of the steel.
